name: ui2code-visual-synthesis
description: Generate executable code from UI designs and screenshots using vision-language models, with test-time scaling for improved quality and accuracy. Automate UI implementation from visual mockups. Triggers: converting UI designs to code, automating web/app development, or building design-to-code systems.
content:
## UI2Code^N: Scalable Visual-to-Code Generation Converting visual UI designs into functional code remains largely manual and error-prone. UI2Code^N demonstrates how vision-language models can generate code from UI screenshots or mockups, with test-time scaling (using multiple inference steps) to improve accuracy and code quality. The ^N indicates scalable quality through inference-time computation. ### Core Concept **Traditional:** Designer → Screenshot → Developer → Code (slow, error-prone) ``` Takes days, requires developer interpretation, prone to implementation errors ``` **UI2Code^N:** Screenshot → VLM → Code (fast, accurate) ``` Minutes, preserves design fidelity, can generate multiple quality variants ``` Key innovation: Test-time scaling - spending more computation at inference to generate higher-quality code without additional training. ### Architecture Overview ``` ┌──────────────────────────┐ │ UI Screenshot/Mockup │ └───────────┬──────────────┘ │ ┌─────▼──────────────┐ │ Vision Encoder │ │ (extract layout, │ │ colors, text) │ └─────┬──────────────┘ │ ┌─────▼──────────────────────┐ │ UI Analysis Head │ │ - Component detection │ │ - Layout parsing │ │ - Property extraction │ └─────┬──────────────────────┘ │ ┌─────▼─────────────────────────────┐ │ Test-Time Sampling │ │ Generate N code candidates │ │ with different strategies: │ │ - Top-down hierarchy │ │ - Grid-based layout │ │ - Flex-box responsive │ └─────┬─────────────────────────────┘ │ ┌─────▼──────────────────┐ │ Code Quality Ranker │ │ - Syntax correctness │ │ - Layout fidelity │ │ - Responsiveness │ └─────┬──────────────────┘ │ ┌─────▼──────────────────┐ │ Best Code Candidate │ │ (with quality score) │ └────────────────────────┘ ``` ### Implementation #### Step 1: UI Analysis Module Extract structured information from visual designs. ```python from dataclasses import dataclass from typing import List, Dict, Any import torch import torch.nn as nn @dataclass class UIComponent: """Parsed UI component""" name: str component_type: str # button, input, card, etc bounding_box: tuple # (x, y, w, h) normalized properties: Dict[str, Any] # color, text, size, etc children: List['UIComponent'] = None class UIAnalyzer(nn.Module): """Extract UI structure from screenshots""" def __init__(self, vision_dim: int = 768): super().__init__() # Component detection self.component_detector = nn.Sequential( nn.Linear(vision_dim, 512), nn.ReLU(), nn.Linear(512, 256), # component class embeddings ) # Layout analyzer self.layout_analyzer = nn.Sequential( nn.Linear(vision_dim, 512), nn.ReLU(), nn.Linear(512, 4), # bounding box regression ) # Property extractor self.property_extractor = nn.Sequential( nn.Linear(vision_dim, 512), nn.ReLU(), nn.Linear(512, 50), # properties: colors, fonts, spacing ) def forward(self, visual_features: torch.Tensor) -> List[UIComponent]: """ Extract UI components from screenshot Args: visual_features: (num_regions, vision_dim) Returns: List of UIComponent objects """ batch_size = visual_features.shape[0] components = [] # Detect components component_logits = self.component_detector(visual_features) component_classes = torch.argmax(component_logits, dim=-1) # Analyze layout bboxes = torch.sigmoid(self.layout_analyzer(visual_features)) # normalize to [0,1] # Extract properties properties = self.property_extractor(visual_features) # Assemble UI components component_names = ["button", "input", "card", "navbar", "text"] for i in range(batch_size): component = UIComponent( name=f"{component_names[component_classes[i]]}_{i}", component_type=component_names[component_classes[i]], bounding_box=tuple(bboxes[i].tolist()), properties={"colors": properties[i, :10].tolist(), "spacing": properties[i, 10:20].tolist()}, ) components.append(component) return components ``` #### Step 2: Multi-Strategy Code Generation Generate code using different strategies and select best result. ```python class CodeGenerator: """Generate code from UI components using multiple strategies""" def __init__(self): self.strategies = ["hierarchical", "grid", "flexbox", "tailwind"] def generate(self, components: List[UIComponent], num_samples: int = 4) -> str: """Generate and select best code""" candidates = [] for strategy in self.strategies[:num_samples]: code = self._generate_with_strategy(components, strategy) quality = self._evaluate_quality(code, components) candidates.append((code, quality, strategy)) # Select best by quality best_code, _, best_strategy = max(candidates, key=lambda x: x[1]) return best_code def _generate_with_strategy(self, components: List[UIComponent], strategy: str) -> str: """Generate code using specific strategy""" if strategy == "hierarchical": return self._generate_hierarchical(components) elif strategy == "grid": return self._generate_grid(components) elif strategy == "flexbox": return self._generate_flexbox(components) else: return self._generate_tailwind(components) def _generate_hierarchical(self, components: List[UIComponent]) -> str: """Top-down hierarchy generation""" html = "
\n" for comp in components: html += self._component_to_html(comp, indent=2) html += "
\n" return html def _generate_grid(self, components: List[UIComponent]) -> str: """CSS Grid layout""" html = "
\n" for comp in components: x, y, w, h = comp.bounding_box style = f"grid-column: {int(x*12)}/{int((x+w)*12)}; " \ f"grid-row: {int(y*12)}/{int((y+h)*12)};" html += f"
\n" html += f" {comp.name}\n" html += "
\n" html += "
\n" return html def _generate_flexbox(self, components: List[UIComponent]) -> str: """Flexbox-based layout""" html = "
\n" for comp in components: html += self._component_to_html(comp, indent=2) html += "
\n" return html def _generate_tailwind(self, components: List[UIComponent]) -> str: """Tailwind CSS generation""" html = "
\n" for comp in components: # Use Tailwind classes classes = self._tailwind_classes(comp) html += f" <{comp.component_type} class='{classes}'>\n" html += f" {comp.name}\n" html += f" \n" html += "
\n" return html def _component_to_html(self, comp: UIComponent, indent: int = 0) -> str: """Convert component to HTML""" prefix = " " * indent return f"{prefix}
{comp.name}
\n" def _tailwind_classes(self, comp: UIComponent) -> str: """Generate Tailwind classes based on component properties""" classes = ["p-4", "rounded", "border"] if comp.component_type == "button": classes.extend(["bg-blue-500", "text-white", "hover:bg-blue-600"]) elif comp.component_type == "input": classes.extend(["border-gray-300", "focus:outline-blue-500"]) return " ".join(classes) def _evaluate_quality(self, code: str, components: List[UIComponent]) -> float: """Evaluate code quality""" # Check syntax score = 0.5 try: # Basic syntax check if code.count('<') == code.count('>'): score += 0.25 except: pass # Check coverage (mention all components) mentions = sum(code.count(c.name) for c in components) coverage = mentions / len(components) score += coverage * 0.25 return score ``` #### Step 3: Responsive Design Generation Enhance generated code with responsive behavior. ```python class ResponsiveDesignEnhancer: """Make generated code responsive across screen sizes""" def enhance(self, html_code: str) -> str: """Add responsive design patterns""" # Add viewport meta tag if "viewport" not in html_code: html_code = self._add_viewport_meta(html_code) # Convert fixed sizes to responsive html_code = self._make_responsive(html_code) # Add breakpoints html_code = self._add_breakpoints(html_code) return html_code def _add_viewport_meta(self, html: str) -> str: """Add viewport configuration""" meta = '\n' return meta + html def _make_responsive(self, html: str) -> str: """Convert fixed to responsive units""" import re # width: 800px; → width: 100%; max-width: 800px; html = re.sub(r'width:\s*(\d+)px', r'width: 100%; max-width: \1px', html) # font-size: 20px; → font-size: clamp(12px, 2vw, 20px) html = re.sub(r'font-size:\s*(\d+)px', r'font-size: clamp(\1px * 0.6, 2vw, \1px)', html) return html def _add_breakpoints(self, html: str) -> str: """Add CSS media queries""" css = """ """ return html + css ``` ### Practical Guidance **When to use UI2Code:** - Converting Figma/Sketch designs to code - Prototyping UI quickly - Generating boilerplate layouts - Automating repetitive UI patterns **Quality improvement strategies:** 1. **Input quality** - High-resolution, well-organized mockups 2. **Component libraries** - Pre-define common components for model 3. **Test-time scaling** - Generate 4-8 candidates, select best 4. **Post-processing** - Add responsive design, optimization **Common issues and fixes:** - **Layout mismatches** - Train on diverse layout types - **Responsive failures** - Always enhance with breakpoints - **Missing interactions** - Use model to generate JavaScript event handlers - **Color inaccuracy** - Extract color palette explicitly in analysis phase **Integration patterns:** - Combine with design systems for consistency - Use with component libraries for reliability - Pair with code formatters and linters - Add human review stage for production code **Scaling considerations:** - Batch processing for multiple designs - Cache component analysis for repeated patterns - Stream code generation for long outputs - Use smaller models for real-time feedback ### Reference Paper: UI2Code^N: A Visual Language Model for Test-Time Scalable Interactive UI-to-Code Generation arXiv: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.08195 **Citation:** ```bibtex @article{ui2code2025, title={UI2Code^N: A Visual Language Model for Test-Time Scalable Interactive UI-to-Code Generation}, journal={arXiv preprint arXiv:2511.08195}, year={2025} } ```
